title: Module 1
content:
BJT: Review of biasing- AC and DC load lines, Q point, fixing of operating
point, biasing stability, stability factors ( 1 Hour)
simple biasing circuit design -various biasing methods of BJT: fixed,
voltage divider, biasing independent of power supply. Thermal stability. ( 1
Hour)
MOSFET: n channel- structure and operation, regions of operation, pinch-
off. Biasing methods: by fixing gate source voltage, fixing gate voltage and
connecting source resistance, using two power supplies, drain-gate feedback
resistor. MOSFET as amplifier. channel length modulation (3 Hours)
SMALL
SIGNAL
ANALYSIS
OF
MOSFET:
voltage
transfer
characteristics, small signal operation-equivalent circuit, hybrid pi model.
CS, CG, CD configurations, expressions for characteristic parameters: input
and output impedance, voltage gain and transconductance. (6 Hours)

title: Module 2
content:
MULTISTAGE AND DIFFERENTIAL AMPLIFIERS:
Multi stage amplifier - Differential amplifier- basic MOSFET differential
pair operation. (3 Hours)
FEEDBACK AMPLIFIERS: Effect of negative feedback on bandwidth,
gain and noise reduction. Voltage, Current, Transconductance, and
Transresistance amplifiers. (5 Hours)
POWER AMPLIFIERS: classifications (basic operation using input output
signal and circuit diagram) and power efficiency - class A (series fed/
transformer coupled) - class B (transformer coupled push pull amplifier,
complementary symmetry) - class C - power amplifier (3 Hours)

title: Module 3
content:
BASIC CIRCUITS ANALYSIS:
Review of fundamental laws: Ohm’s law, resistors in series and parallel
circuits voltage and current division, Source transformation technique, star -
delta transformation (1 Hour)
Kirchoff’s laws - mesh current and node voltage - method of mesh and nodal
analysis (1 Hour)
NETWORK THEOREMS FOR RESISTIVE CIRCUITS WITH
DEPENDENT AND INDEPENDENT SOURCES:
Thevenin’s and Norton theorems, Superposition theorem, Maximum power
transfer theorem, Reciprocity theorem, Millman's theorem. related problems
(8 Hours)
LAPLACE TRANSFORM: Review of
Laplace transform and inverse
Laplace transform of common functions and properties. solution of
differential equations using Laplace transform (1 Hour)

title: Module 4
content:
Steady state And transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits , application
of Laplace transform in RLC circuit analysis.
Analysis of electrical and mechanical systems-Mass Spring Damper
system,Electric motor,Liquid level System  (7 Hours)
TWO PORT NETWORKS: Types Of Two Port Networks- Z , Y, H,
ABCD. Concept of symmetry and reciprocity, Conversions. (4 Hours)
